title: sterculia macropoda
intro: Sterculia macropoda, also known as the Indian butter tree or elephant apple, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of India and Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Sterculia macropoda include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of this plant include extracts, decoctions, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
